question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that triggers a DbError, causing handleError() to return HTTP 503 (SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE) and silently suppress the underlying record-fetch failure, so that the mirror node never re-exports the missing record and the client receives no actionable data about which records are absent?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes an unclassified error (not NotFoundError, InvalidArgumentError, RangeError, DbError, or HttpError) to fall through to defaultStatusCode (500 INTERNAL_ERROR), causing handleError() to return a generic message that masks the true missing-record condition and prevents the caller from knowing a record was not exported?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleUncaughtException()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a non-RestError exception path in handleUncaughtException() that causes the process to crash via re-throw, resulting in in-flight record export operations being aborted and those records never being delivered to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleRejection()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an unhandled promise rejection in handleRejection() that is only logged (logger.warn) but never causes a retry or re-export, so that records involved in the rejected promise are silently dropped from the mirror node export pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: errorMessageFormat()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the _status.messages response structure from errorMessageFormat() to enumerate which specific records are missing from the mirror node by correlating error messages with known record identifiers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldReturnMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldReturnMessage() returns true for all 4xx status codes and BAD_GATEWAY (502), causing raw err.message content (which may include internal record IDs or export state) to be returned in the response, leaking which records were incorrectly or incompletely exported?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes a NotFoundError to be thrown, receiving a 404 response with the raw err.message via shouldReturnMessage(), which discloses whether a specific record was never exported to the mirror node versus simply not yet indexed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: errorMessageFormat()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a response where errorMessageFormat() includes the m.data field (when 'data' is present on the error object), exposing internal record export metadata or partial record content that should not be visible to unauthenticated callers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes an HttpError with an attacker-influenced statusCode to be passed to new StatusCode(err.statusCode, err.msg), resulting in an incorrect HTTP status being returned that misrepresents the export state of a record to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldPrintStacktrace()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an error message containing the string 'statement timeout' to match the simpleErrors regex, suppressing stacktrace logging in shouldPrintStacktrace(), thereby hiding evidence of a failed record export operation from operators who would otherwise investigate and re-export the missing record?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a RangeError (which is mapped to 400 BAD_REQUEST) by supplying boundary-violating input, causing handleError() to return a client-error response that incorrectly attributes a server-side record export failure to the client, masking the true missing-record condition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a next() call in handleError() after res.status().json() to cause the middleware chain to stall, preventing any downstream middleware from triggering a record re-export or correction mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated requests that each trigger a DbError, causing handleError() to return 503 each time without any circuit-breaker or retry logic, resulting in a sustained window during which records are not exported and the mirror node accumulates gaps?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: errorMessageFormat()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a non-array, non-string error message object through errorMessageFormat() that has a 'detail' property, causing the response to include both message and detail fields that together reveal the internal state of a failed record export?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the elapsed time value (Date.now() - startTime) logged by handleError() via timing side-channels in the response, inferring whether a record lookup failed early (missing record) or late (export pipeline delay)?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user spoof the req.ip value (e.g., via X-Forwarded-For) logged in handleError(), causing incorrect attribution in error logs and preventing operators from identifying the true source of requests that trigger missing-record errors?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleUncaughtException()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a sequence of requests that causes handleUncaughtException() to re-throw a non-RestError, crashing the Node.js process and causing all pending record export operations buffered in memory to be lost permanently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes an InvalidArgumentError to be thrown with a message containing sensitive record identifiers, which handleError() then returns verbatim via shouldReturnMessage() → err.message, leaking which record IDs exist in the export pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldReturnMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a BAD_GATEWAY (502) response, which shouldReturnMessage() treats as a client-visible error, causing raw backend error messages (potentially containing upstream export node addresses or record state) to be returned to the unauthenticated caller?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user send a malformed request that causes handleError() to be invoked with an err object lacking a .message property, causing errorMessage to be undefined and res.status().json() to return a malformed response that downstream clients interpret as a successful empty record export?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: errorMessageFormat()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an error whose message is an array (already an array), causing errorMessageFormat() to skip the wrapping step and return multiple error entries in _status.messages, some of which may reference internal record export states that should not be disclosed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DbError to be thrown repeatedly, each time receiving a 503 response, and use the pattern of 503 responses to infer that the mirror node's database is unavailable and records are not being exported during that window, enabling targeted timing attacks on data availability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that handleError() is async but does not await any operations, causing race conditions where res.status().json() is called while another middleware is still processing the same request, resulting in duplicate or corrupted error responses that mask the true record export status?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleUncaughtException()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a FileDecodeError (which extends RestError) to be caught by handleUncaughtException() and only logged (not re-thrown), silently swallowing a file decode failure that represents a corrupted or missing record in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes an HttpError with statusCode 200 or 2xx to be passed to handleError(), causing new StatusCode(err.statusCode, err.msg) to create a success-like status code, and res.status(statusCode.code).json() to return a 200 response for an actual error, hiding a missing-record condition?
